description:
The District Office of Representative Joseph Morelle, NY-25 in Rochester, NY, will be accepting applications for an in-person internship during the Summer Semester 2026. Applications will be accepted March 3rd - April 3rd, 2026.

Interns will learn about the functions of a congressional office, congressional communications, and the legislative process. Ideal candidates should possess excellent communication skills, strong attention to detail, the ability to work in a fast-paced environment, and a good sense of humor. New York State ties are preferred but not required.

Internships are intended for current undergraduate students and current graduate students. For more information on the district internship please visit: https://morelle.house.gov/congressional-internships

To apply for the Spring 2026 District Office Internship, please fill out the google form found here: https://forms.gle/bipy9tt71qn5uuN6A. Spring 2026 DO Internship Application Google Form. Our office will be accepting applications on a rolling basis until April 3rd at 11:59pm EST. Applicants are encouraged to submit their application early.
responsibilities: Completing constituent calls
Writing letters, certificates, and proclamations
Day-to-day office work
Interacting with members of staff in the Rochester office and virtually in the D.C. office
Other duties delegated by members of staff including various constituent case work or work on District-based projects of importance
qualifications: Excellent communication skills
Strong attention to detail
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
Good sense of humor
New York State ties preferred but not required
Current undergraduate students and current graduate students
how_to_apply: Submit the following in one PDF document: Resume, Cover letter, One-page writing sample, Dates of availability. To apply for the Spring 2026 District Office Internship, please fill out the google form found here: https://forms.gle/bipy9tt71qn5uuN6A. Our office will be accepting applications on a rolling basis until April 3rd at 11:59pm EST.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

office: Office of a New York Member of Congress (NY) - Member Name Unresolved in Text
description:
In the Washington, D.C. office, internships run throughout the year based on the semester calendar. Applicants must be based in Washington, D.C. for the duration of the internship.

Our internship program typically runs during these times:

Spring: Mid-January to mid-May; application window opens in September
Summer: Mid-May to mid-August; application window opens in February
Fall: Early September to mid-December; application window opens in June

Ideal candidates should possess excellent written and verbal communications skills, a strong attention to detail, the ability to succeed in a fast-paced work environment, and a good sense of humor. New York State ties are preferred but not required.

Washington, D.C. Internship Responsibilities:
Interns in the Washington, D.C. office play an important role in the daily operations of the office and gain firsthand experience in the legislative process.

Throughout the semester, interns learn about the legislative process and the many other functions of a congressional office.

Full-time paid internships run Monday through Friday from 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m. when Congress is in session, and 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. when not in session (refer to the House Calendar). Part-time internships are scheduled to accommodate students' course schedules, as well as the needs of the office. Part-time interns can expect to receive either pay or academic credit. Interns must be a U.S. citizen and legally eligible to work for the U.S. federal government.

Internships are intended for current undergraduate students, current graduate students, and recent college graduates.
responsibilities: Greeting guests and assisting with front office operations
Answering phones, recording constituent opinions, and correctly processing other inquiries
Conducting Capitol tours for constituents and other guests
Supporting the Legislative Correspondent with sorting mail and drafting constituent letters
Attending hearings and briefings, as requested by senior staff
Researching legislation for the Member and legislative team
Assisting with day-to-day office work and other duties as delegated by members of the staff
Interacting with members of staff in the D.C. office and virtually with the Rochester office
Other administrative tasks as assigned
Compile daily press clips and monitor media coverage of issues relevant to the Member
Draft press releases, media advisories, and statements under staff guidance
Assist with social media content creation and scheduling
Help update the office website and other digital platforms
Maintain press contact lists and track media inquiries
Support logistics for press events and prepare briefing materials for interviews or appearances
Assist with processing and tracking incoming invitations to the Member
Confirm details and logistics for meetings, events, and travel
Help maintain the Member's calendar and coordinate with district staff as needed
Prepare briefing materials for scheduled events or meetings
Support logistics for events, including visitor coordination and follow-up
Assist with schedule adjustments, conflicts, and communications
Track and organize inbound constituent correspondence
Draft responses to constituent letters on legislative issues and policy questions under staff supervision
Research issues raised by constituents to inform responses
Assist the Legislative Correspondent in preparing reports summarizing constituent concerns and trends
qualifications: Excellent written and verbal communications skills
Strong attention to detail
Ability to succeed in a fast-paced work environment
Good sense of humor
New York State ties are preferred but not required
Applicants must be based in Washington, D.C. for the duration of the internship
Must be a U.S. citizen and legally eligible to work for the U.S. federal government
Current undergraduate students, current graduate students, or recent college graduates
how_to_apply: To apply for the Summer 2026 DC Internship, please fill out the google form found here: Summer 2026 DC Internship Application Google Form. Our office will be accepting applications on a rolling basis until 2026-04-03 at 11:59pm EST. Applicants are encouraged to submit their application early.
salary_info: Full-time paid internships; Part-time interns receive either pay or academic credit
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, disability, military status, age, marital status, or parental status.

position_title: Summer D.C. Internship With the Office of Congresswoman Bynum
description: The Office of Congresswoman Janelle Bynum (OR-05) is seeking full-time and part-time interns to join our office in Washington, D.C. The preferred internship will run from May 2026 to mid-late August 2026. Seeking applicants who are motivated to learn more about the legislative process, grow professionally, and engage thoughtfully with staff, constituents, and other lawmakers.
responsibilities: Handle incoming correspondence with poise, professionalism, and promptness
Assist Member and key staff with logistical and administrative duties
Update the office's legislative tracker and perform research on legislative measures
Attend briefings and hearings, conduct research, and draft correspondence and memoranda
Partner with communications team to create speeches, social media posts, and talking points
qualifications: College student in good academic standing
Strong oral and written communication skills
The ability to handle difficult situations with courtesy, tact and compassion
Problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently and use good judgement
Responsible, dependable and willing to learn
how_to_apply: Please fill out the internship application on our website under the "Internships" tab. Please select "Summer" to the question regarding "Desired Session." Applicants are encouraged to apply by March 13th, 2026.
equal_opportunity: All applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including marital or parental status), sexual orientation, gender identity, disability, age, or national origin.

description: The office of Congresswoman Susie Lee is seeking full-time, Summer 2026 Interns for the Washington, D.C. office. Successful candidates work well under deadlines, are organized, hard-working, detail-oriented, have excellent customer service skills, and are team players. Applicants with Nevada ties are strongly preferred.
responsibilities: Answering phones
Managing the front office
Assisting legislative staff with research
Assisting the communications teams with drafting
Performing other tasks as assigned
qualifications: Work well under deadlines
Organized
Hard-working
Detail-oriented
Excellent customer service skills
Team player
Nevada ties strongly preferred
how_to_apply: To apply, please submit the following as one PDF document to NV03Resumes@gmail.com with "Summer 2026 DC Intern - [Last Name], [First Name]" in the subject line: Resume, Cover Letter, 2 Brief Writing Samples. Please submit your application by March 13, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. ET. No phone calls or walk-ins please.

office: House Committee on Veterans' Affairs Democratic Staff
description:
The Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs seeks a press/digital intern for the Summer 2026 semester.

A strong work ethic, the ability to excel in fast-paced environments, and a willingness to assist wherever needed is a must.

The ideal candidate will have strong oral and written communications skills, be detail-oriented, have a familiarity with social media platforms, and have a desire to help veterans and learn about the Committee and the legislative process.

The Committee strongly encourages veterans, minority, women, people with disabilities, LGBTQ+ candidates, and HBCU and MSI students to apply.

This paid, full-time or part-time in-person position is based in Washington, D.C.
responsibilities: Compiling and distributing morning press clips
Tracking media hits for Committee members
Assisting with video shoots
Assisting with creating messaging packets and digital toolkits for the caucus
Writing first drafts of remarks, press releases, and video scripts
Developing social media content
Clipping floor and hearing remarks from members
Providing administrative support, including answering calls and delivering correspondence
Other tasks as assigned
qualifications: Strong work ethic
Ability to excel in fast-paced environments
Willingness to assist wherever needed
Strong oral and written communications skills
Detail-oriented
Familiarity with social media platforms
Desire to help veterans and learn about the Committee and the legislative process
how_to_apply: Please visit https://democrats-veterans.house.gov/opportunities for the application. The deadline to apply is 5 pm ET on 2026-03-20. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Late applications will not be accepted.
equal_opportunity: The Committee strongly encourages veterans, minority, women, people with disabilities, LGBTQ+ candidates, and HBCU and MSI students to apply.

description:

This paid, full-time or part-time, in-person position is based in Washington, D.C.
responsibilities: Conduct legislative and policy research
Draft memos, letters, and other written materials
Attend briefings and hearings
Assist with Committee events

description:
The office of Congressman Juan Ciscomani (AZ-06) is accepting applications for Summer 2026 paid internships in our Washington, D.C. Office.

Ideal candidates are motivated, detail-oriented, and possess a strong work ethic, with the ability to communicate effectively with constituents and staff. This internship offers a valuable opportunity to gain hands-on experience in public service and constituent relations while learning about the day-to-day operations of a congressional office.
responsibilities: Answering phones and responding to constituent inquiries
Greeting visitors and assisting with front office operations
Leading guided tours of the U.S. Capitol
Conducting legislative research and writing memos on policy and local issues for the Member and staff
Assisting with additional administrative and legislative tasks as needed
qualifications: Strong written and verbal communication skills
Willingness to learn and take initiative
Professional demeanor and excellent attention to detail
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively as part of a team
Demonstrated interest in public service, government, or public policy
Arizona residency or connection to the 6th Congressional District preferred
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should submit a resume and brief cover letter outlining their interest and availability to william.wang@mail.house.gov

office: Democratic Staff of the Committee on Rules
description:
The Democratic Staff of the Committee on Rules is seeking full-time, energetic interns for the summer (June - August). Our interns work closely with Committee staff to prepare materials for Committee meetings, conduct research projects, learn about the Rules of the House and the various procedures for legislation to reach the House Floor, and complete a variety of other administrative tasks. Ideal candidates have strong writing skills, a good work ethic, an interest in learning about House floor procedures, and are detail-oriented. Interns will gain experience in legislative analysis, political communications, and institutional processes.

All offers are conditional on completion of a federal criminal background check.
responsibilities: Prepare materials for Committee meetings
Conduct research projects
Learn about the Rules of the House and procedures for legislation to reach the House Floor
Complete administrative tasks
qualifications: Strong writing skills
Good work ethic
Interest in learning about House floor procedures
Detail-oriented
Massachusetts ties are a plus
how_to_apply: Submit a resume and cover letter explaining why you want to join the Democratic staff of the House Rules Committee to democrats-rules.house.gov/about/internships. No calls or drop-ins, please. The deadline to apply is March 15, 2026. Note: Students on quarter systems or with unusual schedules are still encouraged to apply-please detail your particular situation in your cover letter.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and strongly encourages candidates from diverse backgrounds or with untraditional experience to apply.

location: West Chester, Reading, and Washington, D.C.
description:
The Office of Representative Chrissy Houlahan (D-PA-06) is accepting applications for Summer 2026 digital interns in our West Chester, Reading, and Washington, D.C. offices. This internship will run from mid-May through August.

Our office offers part- and full-time internships for students who are in college or a similar post-high school training program, or who have just graduated. We are dedicated to fostering the continued success of our interns beyond their time with our office. Former interns of ours have gone on to work in other offices across Capitol Hill, on state-wide political campaigns, and in various profit and non-profit organizations. We offer needs-based stipends to students who are Pell Grant eligible. Our internship program has returned to in-person work, and we are seeking press interns for our Reading, West Chester, and Washington, D.C. offices.

Ideal intern applicants are adaptable and proactive self-starters who have experience in a fast-paced, deadline-oriented work environment. Digital interns will be responsible for compiling and distributing press clips, helping develop digital content including graphics and videos, and drafting copy for social media and press materials. Digital interns will also have the opportunity to staff the Congresswoman to capture videos and photos of events.

Successful interns in our office are adaptable and proactive self-starters who have experience in a fast-paced, deadline-oriented work environment. Applicants must be independent problem-solvers, have strong writing skills, and be comfortable communicating to both internal and external audiences.

An ideal digital intern has technical experience with video editing software (Premiere, Headliner) and/or graphic design tools (Canva, InDesign, Illustrator). Spanish language skills are preferred but not required. Prior volunteer, leadership, and work experience, ties to PA-06, and experience with photo editing programs (Photoshop, Lightroom) are all a plus.
responsibilities: Compile and distribute press clips
Help develop digital content including graphics and videos
Draft copy for social media and press materials
Staff the Congresswoman to capture videos and photos of events
qualifications: Adaptable and proactive self-starter
Experience in a fast-paced, deadline-oriented work environment
Independent problem-solver
Strong writing skills
Comfortable communicating to both internal and external audiences
Technical experience with video editing software (Premiere, Headliner) and/or graphic design tools (Canva, InDesign, Illustrator)
Spanish language skills preferred but not required
Prior volunteer, leadership, and work experience, ties to PA-06, and experience with photo editing programs (Photoshop, Lightroom) are a plus
how_to_apply: Submit the following to PA06Press.Internships@mail.house.gov: 1) a resume, 2) ONE brief paragraph about yourself and why you're interested in interning in our office, 3) a writing sample, and 4) one example of digital work (video, graphic or photo).
salary_info: Needs-based stipends available for students who are Pell Grant eligible
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender, LGBTQ identification, family status, or military status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.

location: West Chester, Reading, and Washington, D.C.
description:
The Office of Representative Chrissy Houlahan (D-PA-06) is accepting applications for Summer 2026 legislative interns in our West Chester, Reading, and Washington, D.C. offices. This internship will run from mid-May through August.

Both full and part time internships are available, and we offer stipends to applicants, with accommodations for Pell-eligible applicants. Our internship program has returned to in-person work with flexible telework options should interns need to work from home.

Ideal applicants are self-starters who bring a customer service mindset and a passion for community service. Applicants must be intellectually curious, willing to spend a lot of time on the phone and write well.

Responsibilities range from helping constituents, to doing research and drafting memos for our legislative staff, to staffing the Congresswoman in hearings or meetings. We offer interns the opportunity to see Congress up close and from the inside. Prior volunteer, leadership, and work experience are a plus. Ties to PA-06 are also a plus.

Please submit applications through the "Legislative Internship" form on our website, Houlahan.house.gov.

Due to a high volume of applicants, you will likely only be contacted if you are selected for an interview. This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender, LGBTQ identification, family status, or military status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Helping constituents
Doing research and drafting memos for legislative staff
Staffing the Congresswoman in hearings or meetings
qualifications: Self-starter with a customer service mindset and a passion for community service
Intellectually curious
Willing to spend a lot of time on the phone
Strong writing skills
Prior volunteer, leadership, and work experience preferred
Ties to PA-06 preferred
how_to_apply: Submit applications through the "Legislative Internship" form on our website, Houlahan.house.gov.
salary_info: Stipends offered; accommodations for Pell-eligible applicants.
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender, LGBTQ identification, family status, or military status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.

description:
The Washington, D.C. office of Congresswoman Gwen Moore (WI-04) is seeking interns for the upcoming Summer 2026 term.

Interns are paid via a monthly stipend.
responsibilities: Answer phones
Attend hearings and briefings
Draft and edit constituent correspondence letters
Write memos
Conduct research for the legislative staff
Provide support for the day-to-day activities of the office
qualifications: Available on an hourly, daily, and monthly basis during the Summer term
Ability to provide a resume, cover letter, and two short writing samples
how_to_apply: Submit a resume, cover letter, and two short writing samples to WI04DC.Internships@mail.house.gov. Additionally, applicants should indicate their availability on an hourly, daily, and monthly basis during the Summer term.
equal_opportunity: The Office of Congresswoman Moore is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, religion, national origin, age, or socioeconomic status. All are welcome and encouraged to apply.

description: Congressman Mike Levin (CA-49) seeks press and legislative interns for his Washington, D.C. office for the Summer 2026 term. These internships provide an opportunity for undergraduate students, graduate students, and recent graduates to learn more about the workings of a Congressional office and the legislative process. Interns should have a general understanding of the U.S. political system, value open communication, be eager to grow, and bring a can-do attitude. Internships last approximately three to four months and are intended to be an educational experience. Internships are offered for either academic credit or a stipend. Internship applicants must be high school graduates. Current students and recent graduates will be given first priority for the internship program.
responsibilities: Attending committee hearings and congressional briefings
Conducting policy research and drafting legislative materials
Authoring constituent correspondence on a range of policy issues
Answering phones and processing mail
Leading constituents on tours of the Capitol
Working on other administrative and legislative tasks as directed
Assisting the Communications Director and Digital Manager/Press Secretary in drafting and editing social media content, digital graphics, and other digital materials
Drafting press releases, newsletters, remarks, and talking points
Compiling daily press clips and maintaining press lists
Working on other administrative and communications-related tasks as directed
qualifications: High school graduation required
General understanding of the U.S. political system
Value open communication
Eager to grow and bring a can-do attitude
Current students and recent graduates given priority
Strong social media skills (for Press Interns)
Basic familiarity with graphic design and video editing (for Press Interns)
Submission of a sample graphic or similar communications work strongly encouraged (for Press Interns)
Strong ties to the 49th District preferred
how_to_apply: To apply for an internship with Representative Levin, please email a cover letter, resume, three references, and a brief writing sample all in one PDF file, with the file name including internship class, year, last name, and first name (i.e. Summer2026.Doe.Jane.pdf) to CA49.DCInternships@mail.house.gov by March 15. Please specify whether you are applying for the press internship or the legislative internship.
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, gender identity or expression, or sexual orientation. We deeply value the diversity of our employees and encourage candidates from diverse backgrounds to apply.

description:
The Office of Congresswoman Sarah McBride (D-DE) seeks a paid and highly-motivated Communications Intern for Summer 2026 (June-August) based in the Washington, D.C. office.

The Communications Intern works closely with a collaborative and creative Communications team to support a robust communications and digital strategy. The Communications Intern will also support other general office operations as assigned.
responsibilities: Creating timely, engaging, and creative social media content highlighting Member's legislative work, constituent advocacy wins, and community events
Producing high-quality graphics and videos via editing software
Drafting press releases, quotes, and other written materials
Maintaining internal clips lists
Compiling daily news clips
Media monitoring and researching
Supporting the communications, legislative, and scheduling teams with other duties as assigned
Answering phones
Researching legislation for staff
Attending briefings and hearings
qualifications: Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
Keen attention to detail, deadlines, and social media best practices and trends
Previous experience in communications and digital creation
Experience with Adobe Creative Suite, including Premiere Pro, Lightroom, and Photoshop
Experience with Canva
Experience with TVEyes
Experience with Snapstream
how_to_apply: Please send a cover letter, resume, writing sample (2 pages maximum) and 2-3 graphic or video samples in a single PDF to press.mcbride@mail.house.gov with the subject line "Communications Intern Summer 2026 - Last Name." Applications close March 11, 2026.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, national origin, age, disability, military status, marital or parental status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.
